meta.title: Document Depot
meta.subtitle: Central library for carriers, compliance, marketing, and leadership resources.

emailProfiles.title: Leadership & agent email setup
emailProfiles.description: One published mail settings file feeds both Apple devices and Android: Android users get a guided, copy-friendly walkthrough built from the same technical fields (servers, ports, security). Replace pending entries by uploading the mail settings file from your provider and setting status to "live" in documents.json.
emailProfiles.iphoneHelp: On iPhone or iPad: tap your profile link → Allow → open Settings → Profile Downloaded → Install. You may need your device passcode. There is no safe generic script that injects credentials; the profile file from your provider is the supported method.
emailProfiles.androidHelp: Android (e.g. Galaxy S24) does not use .mobileconfig. Use Samsung Email or Outlook with the server settings sheet, or follow your provider’s Android steps. Exchange accounts often auto-discover when you enter email and password in Outlook.

androidFlipTutorial.title: Kevin — Samsung Galaxy S24 email setup
androidFlipTutorial.subtitle: Each screen has everything for that step. Use Back and Next — works on your phone, tablet, or a laptop beside your phone.

steps.title: Welcome, Kevin
steps.body: This guide is for setting up **work email** on your **Samsung Galaxy S24** (the same ideas work on other Android phones too). You can read each step on this screen or on a **computer** while you work on your phone.
steps.tip: A **later step** may list **server names and ports** from your organization’s published mail file—use those when Outlook or Samsung Email asks for manual details.

steps.title: Install Microsoft Outlook
steps.body: Open the **Google Play Store** → search **Microsoft Outlook** (publisher: **Microsoft Corporation**) → tap **Install**. Outlook is usually the simplest path for **Microsoft 365** or **Exchange** mail.
steps.tip: Samsung’s own mail app is fine too — we cover it in a later step.

steps.title: Add your work account
steps.body: Open **Outlook** → **Add account** (or **Get started**) → choose **Office 365** or **Exchange** if you see those options → enter your **TPG work email address** and continue.
steps.tip: If you’re not sure of your exact work address, ask the team — it may differ from a personal email you use.

steps.title: Password and sign-in security
steps.body: Enter your mailbox **password** when prompted. Complete **multi-factor authentication** (Authenticator app, text code, etc.) if your organization requires it.
steps.tip: If sign-in keeps failing, check that the phone’s **date and time** are automatic, and try **Wi‑Fi** instead of mobile data.

steps.title: If the app asks for server details
steps.body: Some accounts need a **server hostname** or **advanced** settings. Use the **copy-and-paste values** from the step that lists your organization’s mail settings, or open **Guided setup** from the Document Depot on another tab.
steps.tip: Never guess passwords — use your company’s password reset or IT if you’re locked out.

steps.title: Samsung Email (optional)
steps.body: Instead of Outlook: **Settings → Accounts and backup → Accounts → Add account → Exchange** (wording can vary by Android version). Use the **same email and server values** your organization provided.
steps.tip: Look for **Exchange**, **Microsoft**, or **Corporate** if you don’t see a perfect match.

steps.title: You’re all set
steps.body: Mail often appears within a few minutes. In Outlook, **pull down** on the inbox to refresh. On a **Galaxy S24**, you can also check notification settings if new mail doesn’t alert you.
steps.tip: Still stuck? Take a **screenshot** of any error and reach out to **technical support** — that’s what we’re here for.
